title: phymatopteris conjuncta

parts: Phymatopteris conjuncta has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and rhizomes. The leaves of Phymatopteris conjuncta contain flavonoids, such as quercetin, and phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots and rhizomes of the plant are rich in triterpenoids, including ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. These medicinal parts have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
side_effects: Phymatopteris conjuncta can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal irritation, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Ingesting the plant's leaves or stems can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while skin contact may lead to redness, itching, and blistering. In severe cases, improper use of Phymatopteris conjuncta can trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction. If you suspect exposure to this plant, it is essential to seek medical attention immediately.
